mainmatter.p: We fix pairs \mathopen {} \left ( a,b \right ) \mathclose {} and \mathopen {} \left ( a',b' \right ) \mathclose {} in \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx}. By means of the total path object on the pointwise displayed path object on B, it suffices to exhibit identifications p \colon a = a' and p_* b = b'. These are given respectively by our assumptions that A and Ba' are propositions.

If A is a proposition and B \colon A \to \mathsf {Type} is a proposition, then the dependent sum \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx} is a proposition.
A type A is said to be a proposition when every x:A, the type A is contractible with centre x, i.e. all elements of A are identified.

frontmatter.title: The intrinsic opcleaving of a family of path objects

mainmatter.p: Given a type A and a family of path objects B \colon A \to \mathsf {PathObj}, we can always define a opcleaving rendering B fibrant over the intrinsic path object on A. The opcleaving is obtained directly from the transport operation of the identification type.

frontmatter.title: The total path object of a displayed path object

mainmatter.p: Let A be a path object, and let B: \mathsf {DispPathObj} \, A be a displayed path object structure over A. The total path object of B over A, written A.B, is defined as follows:

Reflexivity data is given by assigning 1_{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,u \right ) \mathclose {} } : \equiv \mathopen {} \left ( 1_{ x } , 1_{ u } \right ) \mathclose {}. Univalence is established in two singleton inductions, first using univalence of \mathopen {} \left ( \cong _A \right ) \mathclose {} followed by univalence of \mathopen {} \left ( \cong _B^ \bullet \right ) \mathclose {}.
